title: hadrangis cadetii
intro: Hadrangis cadetii, also known as Kadu, is a medicinal plant native to the tropical forests of India. It is used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its numerous health benefits, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are known to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Hadrangis cadetii is typically prepared as an infusion, decoction, or powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
